title: legnephora moorei
intro: Legnephora moorei, also known as the "Queensland nut" or "bunya nut", is a species of evergreen tree native to Australia, producing edible seeds. The seeds of the Legnephora moorei tree have been used in traditional medicine, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, due to their expectorant properties. The medicinal properties of the seeds also suggest potential health benefits in reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the seeds are often dried and ground into a powder or used whole, either as a supplement or in traditional remedies such as teas and infusions.
